page: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by a shared AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 1 vCPU consisting of 1 physical core and 1 thread, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes 40 GB of local SSD storage, with no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying virtualization is managed via the QEMU hypervisor.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier at 2999.88, while multi-core performance is weak at the same score due to the single-core limitation. Memory performance is generally low, with read-only small block bandwidth at 146.65 GB/sec and a PassMark memory score of 954.92. However, write bandwidth for large blocks is average at 28.87 GB/sec, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both perform at average levels.

Given the shared CPU allocation and limited 2.0 GB memory capacity, this server is positioned as a highly resource-dense, budget-friendly option. It is not suited for memory-intensive databases or high-throughput web serving, as evidenced by its low PassMark database operations score and static web serving throughput of 2.11 GB/sec. It is best utilized for basic utility scripts, lightweight single-threaded tasks, microservices, or development and testing environments where resource demands are minimal.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring a shared AMD EPYC Genoa processor clocked at 2.0 GHz with 1 vCPU, 1 physical core, and 2.0 GB of RAM. The server is provisioned with 40 GB of local SSD storage and runs on the QEMU hypervisor, without any integrated GPUs. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the lower percentiles. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU resources, this instance offers a highly cost-efficient profile for low-demand applications. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, microservices, development environments, and basic utility workloads rather than high-throughput web serving or database operations.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring a shared AMD EPYC Genoa CPU, 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 40 GB of local SSD storage, optimized for budget-friendly, lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is a budget-friendly x86_64 virtual server with 1 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 40 GB local SSD storage.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual server featuring AMD EPYC Genoa architecture and local SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a shared AMD EPYC Genoa processor at 2.0 GHz
Equipped with 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 40 GB local SSD storage
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Features hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and cryptography instruction sets
Best suited for lightweight microservices, development, and utility tasks
